doctrine_basis: Ticket-pack planning must not modify repo truth to make tickets executable.
Schema/runtime/contracts/scaffold changes require separate control implementation PRs.
Codex must not invent schema fields, APIs, service boundaries, permissions, events, ADL meaning, runtime behavior, or business rules.

what_is_derivable.tenant_scoping_policy: Every new Phase 6B model must be tenant scoped through organization_id.
what_is_derivable.registry_metadata_policy: Every new Phase 6B model must be represented in entity registry metadata.

what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.question: Should P6B-SCHEMA-002 create technical scaffold tables only, or full business fields for each model?
what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.why_it_matters: A technical scaffold table with only id, organization_id, timestamps, and registry metadata may pass Prisma/registry validation but will not make capability tickets fully executable. Full business fields would require field semantics that are not fully specified in the accepted contract.

what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.question: Are generic Json payload/metadata fields allowed as schema-control placeholders?
what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.why_it_matters: Generic Json fields can avoid inventing business columns, but they may hide future business logic and weaken executable-ticket exactness.

what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.question: Should Phase 6B CRM intake/dedup/pipeline models link structurally to existing LeadRecord, or remain independent Phase6B* scaffold tables until capability tickets define relations?
what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.why_it_matters: The repo already has LeadRecord, LeadStatusHistory, and LeadAssignmentHistory. Adding Phase6B CRM tables without relation policy may duplicate authority or create stale tickets.

what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.question: Should finance models include cross-model relations in P6B-SCHEMA-002, or should all relations wait for capability-specific tickets?
what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.why_it_matters: Invoices, receivables, payments, allocations, receipts, top-ups, reconciliation candidates, GL, banking, payroll, and billing have natural relations, but relation cardinality is not fully specified by the model contract.

what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.question: What is the approved migration baseline shape for Phase 6B: model existence only, relation skeleton, or full persistence schema?
what_is_not_derivable_without_schema_dec.why_it_matters: The migration SQL must be non-destructive and reviewable. Choosing the wrong baseline shape either under-unblocks capability tickets or invents schema semantics.

forbidden_actions_until_decision: Do not add Phase 6B Prisma models.
Do not create Phase 6B migration SQL.
Do not update entity registry metadata for Phase 6B models.
Do not generate registry output for Phase 6B models.
Do not promote Phase 6B capability tickets to executable status.

recommended_next_control_ticket.title: Approve Phase 6B schema baseline field policy before Prisma implementation
recommended_next_control_ticket.minimum_concrete_requirement: A machine-readable field-level schema policy states whether P6B-SCHEMA-002 creates technical scaffold tables only, generic Json payload scaffolds, relation skeletons, or full persistence fields for each Phase 6B model.
